task_completion.description: Open the tag register and process standards specification document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document viewer (e.g., Notepad, Word, PDF reader) is open showing the tag register and process standards from the Desktop\CrimsonTasks folder.

task_completion.description: Create the 7 active Line-1 process monitoring tags in Crimson 3.0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Crimson 3.0 Navigation Pane under 'Data Tags' shows the newly created tags: TT_601, VM_601, FT_601, CT_601, PT_601, TT_602, and AT_601.

task_completion.description: Set the Float data type, engineering ranges, unit labels, and descriptions for the tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Crimson 3.0 properties pane for a selected tag, the Data Type is set to 'Floating Point' (or Float), and the Data Limits (min/max), Label (units), and Description fields are filled out according to the specification.

task_completion.description: Set the alarm thresholds for the active tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Alarms' tab in the tag properties pane is visible with configured alarm thresholds for at least one of the active tags.
The tags 'CM_602' and 'BW_602' are visibly absent from the Data Tags list in the Crimson 3.0 Navigation Pane.

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'cement_kiln_rawmill.c3' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\' and filename 'cement_kiln_rawmill.c3', OR the Crimson 3.0 main window title bar displays 'cement_kiln_rawmill.c3'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Crimson 3.0 GUI to configure the tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Crimson 3.0 interface being actively used (menus, properties panes, navigation tree), rather than a text editor modifying the raw .c3 file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts to bypass manual configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or IDEs are shown running Python/PowerShell scripts to automatically generate the Crimson project file.

integrity.description: The agent actually created the tags in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tags appear inside the Crimson 3.0 Data Tags tree, not just typed out in a separate notepad or text document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Active tags to verify: TT_601 (800-1600 Degrees Celsius), VM_601 (0-30 Millimeters per Second RMS), FT_601 (0-500 Metric Tonnes per Hour), CT_601 (0-1000 Amperes), PT_601 (0-100 Millibar), TT_602 (200-1000 Degrees Celsius), AT_601 (0-2000 Milligrams per Normal Cubic Meter). All must be Float data type. Inactive tags to exclude: CM_602, BW_602. Save path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\cement_kiln_rawmill.c3
